title: Peloton Interactive Inc (PTON) Q3 2020 Earnings Call Transcript
text: Peloton Q3 2020 Earnings Call Summary. Key Financial Highlights: Peloton reported strong Q3 2020 results, with CEO John Foley noting that the company achieved its first adjusted EBITDA positive quarter as a public company in Q3 with an adjusted EBITDA margin of 4.5%. Revenue grew 66% percent year-over-year to $524.6 million. The company raised full-year guidance to $1.72-1.74 billion in revenue, up from previous projections of $1.53-1.55 billion. Subscriber Growth and Engagement: Connected Fitness Subscribers reached over 886,000, representing 94% year-over-year growth. The company logged 44.2 million workouts during the quarter, up 145% from the prior year period. CFO Jill Woodworth highlighted that our Connected Fitness product gross margin was 45.3% exceeding our expectations, driven by product mix improvements and logistics efficiencies. COVID-19 Impact and Digital Strategy: The pandemic created significant tailwinds for the business. Peloton extended its digital subscription free trial from 30 to 90 days, resulting in over 1.1 million downloads within six weeks. Supply Chain Challenges: President William Lynch acknowledged that the sudden demand surge created supply constraints. The company more than doubled bike production output compared to early March levels and brought a new manufacturing facility online in Taiwan to support future growth. Strategic Outlook: Management emphasized this isn't purely a pandemic story. Foley stated the company is entering its sixth consecutive year of growing our subscriber count by more than 100%, suggesting sustainable long-term growth beyond COVID-19 related tailwinds.
